2604.19548.title: Taming Actor-Observer Asymmetry in Agents via Dialectical Alignment
2604.19548.paper_summary: Large Language Model agents have rapidly evolved from static text generators into dynamic systems capable of executing complex autonomous workflows. To enhance reliability, multi-agent frameworks assigning specialized roles are increasingly adopted to enable self-reflection and mutual auditing. While such role-playing effectively leverages domain expert knowledge, we find it simultaneously induces a human-like cognitive bias known as Actor-Observer Asymmetry (AOA). Specifically, an agent acting as an actor (during self-reflection) tends to attribute failures to external factors, whereas an observer (during mutual auditing) attributes the same errors to internal faults. We quantify this using our new Ambiguous Failure Benchmark, which reveals that simply swapping perspectives triggers the AOA effect in over 20% of cases for most models. To tame this bias, we introduce ReTAS (Reasoning via Thesis-Antithesis-Synthesis), a model trained through dialectical alignment to enforce perspective-invariant reasoning. By integrating dialectical chain-of-thought with Group Relative Policy Optimization, ReTAS guides agents to synthesize conflicting viewpoints into an objective consensus. Experiments demonstrate that ReTAS effectively mitigates attribution inconsistency and significantly improves fault resolution rates in ambiguous scenarios.
2604.19548.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型代理已迅速從靜態文本生成器演變為能夠執行複雜自主工作流程的動態系統。為了增強可靠性，越來越多地採用多代理框架來分配專門角色，以實現自我反思和相互審核。雖然這種角色扮演有效利用了領域專家的知識，但我們發現它同時引發了一種類似人類的認知偏誤，稱為行為者-觀察者不對稱（AOA）。具體而言，作為行為者的代理（在自我反思期間）傾向於將失敗歸因於外部因素，而作為觀察者的代理（在相互審核期間）則將相同的錯誤歸因於內部故障。我們使用新的模糊失敗基準來量化這一點，該基準顯示，僅僅交換視角就會在大多數模型中觸發超過20%的AOA效應。為了駕馭這種偏見，我們引入了ReTAS（通過論題-反論題-綜合推理），這是一種通過辯證對齊訓練的模型，以強制執行視角不變的推理。通過將辯證思維鏈與群體相對政策優化相結合，ReTAS指導代理將相互矛盾的觀點綜合成客觀共識。實驗表明，ReTAS有效減輕了歸因不一致性，並顯著提高了在模糊場景中的故障解決率。
2604.19548.author: Bobo Li et.al.
2604.19548.authors: Bobo Li, Rui Wu, Zibo Ji, Meishan Zhang, Hao Fei, Min Zhang, Mong-Li Lee, Wynne Hsu
